course_title: Fundamentals of English to Chinese Translation in the Age of AI

learning_outcomes.description: Identify and discuss basic translation theories and principles.
Analyse and apply different translation techniques in the translation process.
Interpret an English source text in an accurate and culturally appropriate manner.
Apply different translation approaches and techniques appropriately to different genres and styles appropriate to the translation industry.
Translate common genres from English into idiomatic Chinese in a linguistically and culturally appropriate manner that meets the expectations of translation clients and employers alike.

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a 10-minute online weekly pre-class task on Blackboard (Ultra). The weekly e-task is located in the pre-class folder of each week. The deadline for these tasks is 12pm on the day of the first scheduled class of each week.

  • The Week 1 e-task will be extended to 12pm on Friday of Week 1, after Week 1 classes are all completed.
  • The Week 10 e-task will be extended to 12pm on Tuesday of this week, due to Monday public holiday.

IMPORTANT NOTE: Please note there is ONE additional e-task for in-semester exam revision, located in Assessment Task 2 folder, due on ONE day before the in-semester exam and TWO additional e-tasks for final exam revision, located in the Week 13 folder and due on 26th and 30th of October respectively in Week 13.

ONLY one attempt of each e-task will be permitted. Each e-task will be worth 2% and will test students' knowledge and understanding of the pre-class 'flipped' materials for that week. The best 10 e-task results for each student will be used to calculate the total mark for this assessment item.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester assessment will test students' knowledge and understanding of all the W1-W7 course content, including pre-class, in-class and post-class materials, as well as weekly e-tasks. The assessment may include multiple choice questions, multiple answer questions, matching questions and short answer questions. Students may have access to lecture notes and handouts during the exam. 

The exam will be designed as an online timed assessment on Blackboard Ultra and will be scheduled with a strict time limit so that all students will complete the exam simultaneously.

Students will complete the exam online and must ensure that they have an appropriate device and sufficient internet connection to login to Blackboard Ultra and access the exam.

The exam will be timetabled by the School on Thursday of Week 7. The exam will start at 5:00 pm, with 10 minutes preparation time + 60 minutes working time = 70 minutes. This is a timed activity that students can access via Blackboard (Ultra) link. Answers are automatically submitted at the designated end time.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is a timed activity that students can access via Blackboard (Ultra) link. Answers are automatically submitted at the designated end time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The final translation exam will test student's knowledge, understanding and application of the course content over the whole semester, including pre-class, in-class and post-class materials, with a particular focus on Week 6-12 content. The exam will consist of a number of passages in English to be translated into Chinese. The texts will represent the different genres studied in the course (i.e. news, law, business, science & technology, literature). MT versions (e.g. Google Translate, Baidu, Youdao, DeepL) or AI versions of the translation tasks will be provided in the exam. Students will be required to: 1) translate the source text into the target language on their own; 2) write a brief comment, identifying and explaining errors in the MT/AI versions. Students may have access to a paper dictionary and one specified online dictionary during the exam, as well as lecture notes and materials on Ultra course site.

The exam will be designed as an online timed Inspera assessment. Students will complete the online exam in-person on campus and must ensure that they have an appropriate device to login to Inspera and access the exam. The exam will be timetabled by UQ Central Examinations and administered (invigilated) by the School. Students will be notified of the exact time and date for the Final Exam as soon as the exam timetable is published by UQ Central Examinations. The exam will be 100 minutes in total, which includes 10 minutes planning time + 90 minutes working time.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is a timed exam on UQ Inspera platform that students can access via an Ultra link in Assessment folder. Answers are automatically submitted at the designated end time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
